titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to the Secretary, General Post Office, London, 1 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926) assistant under-secretary to Lord Lieutenant of Ireland Sir Ivor Churchill Guest, Lord Wimborne (1973-1939). The letter provides a reference on behalf of a pupil William Anthony Murray (b. 1880), Atlantic Wireless School, Cahirciveen seeing no reason why he should not be permitted to receive instruction in wireless telegraphy.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO RP/1916/8872/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
1st June, 1916.
8872 Sir, In forwarding the attached papers in the
case of WILLIAM ANTHONY MURRAY, a pupil at the
Atlantic Wireless College, Cahirciveen, I am
directed by the Lords Justices to acquaint you,
for the information of the Postmaster- General,
that Their Excellencies see no reason why this
pupil should not be permitted to receive in-
struction in wireless telegraphy. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, E o F The Secretary,
General Post Office,
London.
